title: The Anointing and the Tomb
subtitle: The hour of Compline: His body wrapped, carried, and laid in the new sepulchre

stillness: It is the hour of Compline.
The day is failing; the Sabbath presses near.
gospel: They took therefore the body of Jesus,
and Joseph wrapped it in a clean linen cloth,
and bound it with linen bands,
with the napkin laid upon His head,
and with spices, as is the manner of the Jews to bury.
Now in the place where He was crucified there was a garden,
and in the garden a new tomb,
wherein no man yet had been laid.
There, because of the Parasceve of the Jews,
for the tomb was near at hand,
they laid Jesus, and buried Him.
And Joseph rolled a great stone to the door of the tomb.
behold: Attend to each thing as if you were present.
Behold how, the hour growing late, John begs Our Lady
to permit the body of Our Lord to be made ready and buried
before the Sabbath comes, in which such things are not lawful.
She, gracious and discerning, would contend no longer,
and signing and blessing the venerable body, let them prepare it.
Behold the Lady: she always held His head in her lap,
which she had kept for herself to make ready;
and Magdalene the feet, where she had found mercy.
See them weep so bitterly that scarcely one could speak.
They looked upon the Mother, stripped of all comfort,
and wept more over her than over their dead Lord.
Then the Lady, seeing she could delay no longer,
lays her face upon the face of her Son,
and from the flood of her tears washes His face
far more than Magdalene had washed His feet;
she wipes it, and kissing His mouth, wraps Him in a cloth.
And all adoring, on bended knees, kissing His feet,
they take Him up and carry Him to the tomb,
fifty paces distant from Mount Calvary:
the Lady held the head, Magdalene the feet,
and the rest were between.
Begin again your own lament, if perhaps you had let it fall.
consider: See the abundance of poverty He took up for us.
In life He had no house of His own;
in death He is laid in another man's tomb,
and, lying naked, is covered by Joseph.
So poor was Christ that He was born not in His own house but a stranger's;
and living in this world, had nowhere to lay His head;
nor dying, anything to cover His nakedness;
nor dead, anything to be wrapped in,
nor tomb nor place where His body might be laid.
There was in His burial no gold, no jewels, no silk,
but linen pure and clean.
The pomp of funerals, the throng of mourners,
the costly care of burial, the rich building of monuments —
these are some comfort to the living, no help to the dead.
The grief that Mary bore at her Son's burial,
David long ago foreshadowed at the burial of Abner:
following the bier, the king wept,
and stirred up others also to weep.
So the blessed Virgin and the devout disciples
bewailed the funeral of Christ,
and tore their inmost hearts with compassion.
While angels in their thousands sang His obsequies,
Mary gave only the groans and sighs of her heart.
prayer: who at the hour of Compline willed to be anointed and prepared with spices,
to be wrapped and bound in the shroud and the other linens,
and, while Your Mother and Your other friends lamented,
to be carried to burial and laid in the tomb:
that I may anoint You with spices, through fervent devotion and a good life;
that I may wrap You in shroud and linens, through pure affection and cleanness of conscience;
that I may mourn You with the tears of penitence and compassion,
and carry You in the arms of charitable and humble labor;
and that I may bury You in my heart, through unceasing remembrance and memory;
so that I too, buried together with You,
may deserve to come with You to the glory of the resurrection. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Sequere et tu pretiosissimum cœli terræque thesaurum; et porta vel pedes vel manus, brachiaque sustenta; terræ defluentes minutatim pretiosissimi sanguinis guttas curiosius collige; et pulverem pedum ejus linge.
latinJewel.english: Follow, you too, the most precious treasure of heaven and earth; carry either His feet or His hands, and bear up His arms; gather up with care the drops of most precious blood that fall little by little upon the ground; and kiss the dust of His feet.
